description: Ever Given ran aground and fully blocked the Suez Canal, halting two-way transit and creating a large vessel backlog that rippled through Europe and Asia trade lanes.
Missile and drone attacks on commercial shipping in the Red Sea and Bab el-Mandeb caused major liners to suspend Suez transits and reroute around the Cape of Good Hope.
Low water levels in Gatun Lake forced the Panama Canal Authority to cut daily transit slots and impose draft restrictions, reducing canal capacity for months.
Pandemic-era demand surges, labor shortages, quarantine rules, and inland logistics bottlenecks triggered sustained global port congestion, long queues, and schedule unreliability.
Insurance verification checks and administrative controls created long vessel queues through the Turkish Straits, slowing crude and product tanker movements in and out of the Black Sea.
Border disruption, weather, and downstream port delays created congestion in the English Channel and Dover approaches, delaying ferries, feeders, and short-sea shipping.
Large-scale diversion of Asia-Europe traffic around the Cape of Good Hope lengthened voyage cycles, tightened vessel supply, and shifted congestion toward African bunkering and relay ports.
